channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Debugging a 400 Error in Sign in with Apple Token Exchange
item.description: Today, I submitted a new build of my app to App Store Connect. While working on the Apple Sign In integration for this Flutter project, I ran into a server-side issue during the token exchange...

item.title: Reachy Mini Not Speaking
item.description: Reachy Mini Not Speaking in Reachy Mini Conversation App— GStreamer DeviceMonitor Fails Without PipeWire/PulseAudio Before you jump in Make sure to check that you have not run out OpenAI api...

item.title: How I Designed a Content Distribution Pipeline — 9 Content Types, 20+ Platforms, One Interactive Diagram
item.description: The Copy-Paste Problem Every time I publish a dev.to article, I share it on X and Mastodon — but why not also Bluesky and Threads? I keep meaning to, but it's manual work every time. My podcast...

item.title: How to Build Website Uptime Monitoring in 10 Minutes (Free, No Dependencies)
item.description: You don't need Datadog, PagerDuty, or a $29/month plan to monitor your websites. With 40 lines of code and a free API, you can build uptime monitoring that actually alerts you when things break....

item.title: How I applied SOLID principles and Interface patterns in AL to build an AppSource-ready BC extension
item.description: Most AL developers have written this at least once: alcase Strategy of Standard: Score := CalculateStandard(...); Weighted: Score := CalculateWeighted(...); end; It works. Until a third strategy....

item.title: Setting Up a Raspberry Pi Homelab Server: A Comprehensive Guide for Intermediate to Advanced Users
item.description: Setting Up a Raspberry Pi Homelab Server: A Comprehensive Guide for Intermediate to Advanced Users In this article, you'll learn how to build a low-power, cost-effective homelab server using a...

item.title: Testing Email Flows in CI/CD with Temporary Email APIs
item.description: Email confirmation is one of the most common flows in modern web applications, and one of the most reliably broken in automated test suites. Registration flows, password resets, magic links, and...

item.title: The Weather Says Clear Skies. So Why Is the Trail a Swamp?
item.description: I love mountain biking. I love skateboarding. I love pretty much anything that involves wheels and dirt and concrete and the outdoors. What I don't love is driving 30 minutes to a trailhead only...

item.title: Rise of Agentic AI
item.description: This paper that i just read provides a detailed review of agentic Al , a paradigm shift moving AL from the typical reactive tool to autonomous ,goal-driven systems. Unlike the typical generative...

item.title: The Silent Behavioral Shift: Why GPT-5.4 Exposes the UI's Fragile Dependence on Backend Semantics
item.description: The release of GPT-5.4 isn't just another incremental LLM update; it's a stark reminder of a fundamental blind spot in our observability stacks. While the headlines focus on new capabilities,...

item.title: Amazon.com is up and down, with login errors and prices not loading
item.description: If you're having issues shopping on Amazon or loading your playlists on Amazon Music, you're not alone. Downdetector is showing a sizable spike in people reporting issues with checkout, search,...

item.title: It’s official: The Pentagon has labeled Anthropic a supply chain risk
item.description: The Department of Defense has officially labeled Anthropic a supply chain risk, making the AI firm the first American company with the label. Meanwhile, the DOD continues to use Anthropic's AI in...

item.title: United Airlines can permanently ban passengers who don’t wear headphones
item.description: United Airlines has updated its "Contract of Carriage" to include a line that requires passengers to wear headphones while listening to audio and video content on flights, CBS News reports. Under...

item.title: Microsoft teases its next Xbox, says ‘Project Helix’ will play PC games too
item.description: The next-generation Xbox console will play both Xbox and PC games, and its codename is "Project Helix," according to Asha Sharma, who took over as Microsoft's gaming CEO last month. "Great start...

item.title: Birdbuddy’s AI-powered hummingbird feeder is matching its best price to date
item.description: Spring is peak bird-watching season, and if you want a closer look this year, Birdbuddy’s Smart Hummingbird Feeder Pro Solar has you covered. Normally $299, Birdbuddy is now selling the...

item.title: Trump gets data center companies to pledge to pay for power generation
item.description: With no enforcement and questionable economics, it may not make a difference.

item.title: Netflix is buying Ben Affleck’s AI startup
item.description: Though Netflix lost the war for Warner Bros., it has just bought an AI startup from the internet's favorite Dunkin' Donuts aficionado. Today, Netflix announced that it has acquired InterPositive,...

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Luma launches creative AI agents powered by its new ‘Unified Intelligence’ models
item.description: Luma introduced Luma Agents, powered by its new “Unified Intelligence” models, designed to coordinate multiple AI systems and generate end-to-end creative work across text, images, video and audio.

item.title: OpenAI launches GPT-5.4 with Pro and Thinking versions
item.description: GPT-5.4 is billed as "our most capable and efficient frontier model for professional work."

item.title: Ask a Techspert: How does AI understand my visual searches?
item.description: Mobile phone with a search bar that says "Ask anything"

item.title: OpenAI’s new GPT-5.4 model is a big step toward autonomous agents
item.description: OpenAI is launching GPT-5.4, the latest version of its AI model that the company says combines advancements in reasoning, coding, and professional work involving spreadsheets, documents, and...

item.title: Israel Hacked Traffic Cameras in Iran
item.description: Multiple news outlets are reporting on Israel’s hacking of Iranian traffic cameras and how they assisted with the killing of that country’s leadership. The New York Times has an <a...

item.title: Cursor is rolling out a new kind of agentic coding tool
item.description: Called Automations, the new system gives users a way to automatically launch agents within their coding environment, triggered by a new addition to the codebase, a Slack message, or a simple timer.

item.title: Roblox is censoring chats with AI
item.description: Roblox is using AI to alter the content of chat messages on its platform in real time using a new feature rolling out today. Real-time chat rephrasing goes beyond the current filtering for banned...

item.title: Meta sued over AI smart glasses’ privacy concerns, after workers reviewed nudity, sex, and other footage
item.description: Lawyers say Meta's marketing materials promised privacy and user control over sharing footage. But an investigation found that subcontractors are reviewing footage from customers' glasses.

item.title: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei could still be trying to make a deal with Pentagon
item.description: Anthropic's $200 million contract with the Department of Defense broke down due to disagreements over giving the military unrestricted access to its AI.

item.title: Meta’s AI glasses reportedly send sensitive footage to human reviewers in Kenya
item.description: Meta's AI-powered smart glasses could be sending sensitive footage to human reviewers in Nairobi, Kenya, according to an investigation by the Swedish outlets Svenska Dagbladet and...

item.title: The latest AI news we announced in February
item.description: an MP4 of a carousel with images reading "Gemini 3.1 Pro" and "Nano Banana 2"

item.title: Osmo is trying to crack AR edutainment (again)
item.description: This is Lowpass by Janko Roettgers, a newsletter on the ever-evolving intersection of tech and entertainment, syndicated just for The Verge subscribers once a week. I still remember the first time...

item.title: Netflix buys Ben Affleck’s AI filmmaking company InterPositive
item.description: InterPositive isn't trying to make AI actors or synthetic performances. Rather, the company has created a model that helps production teams work with footage from their own productions to help...
